signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ Policy Shock
signals.text: The RBNZ raised the Official Cash Rate by 25 bps to 2.50% and flagged further hikes to combat persistent inflation risks. This hawkish guidance directly contradicts speculative positioning, where COT LF net short stands at -25,757 (only 19.5% long), creating an acute long-unwind and short-squeeze risk. The policy tightening reduces the USD/NZD carry advantage to +125bp on a nominal basis, while the NZD real rate remains accommodative at -0.83%. NZD/USD at 0.5697 faces immediate short-covering pressure targeting 0.5800 as specs are forced to cover. Watch for follow-through buying or further hawkish RBNZ commentary to confirm the squeeze.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: AUD/JPY is the primary vehicle for this week's dominant G10 bilateral theme, with AUD outperforming as the weekly leader (+0.653% avg across 4 pairs) and JPY lagging as the weakest currency (-0.528% avg across 7 pairs). The bilateral spread of 1.181% is driven by a massive +337bp OIS carry premium favoring AUD (AONIA 4.35% vs TONA 0.98%). Hawkish comments from RBA's Hunter emphasizing that supply shocks cannot be ignored and that the board will intervene to curb inflation reinforce the AUD bid. Meanwhile, BoJ's Asada dampened JPY support by stating demand-driven inflation is required before another hike. AUD/JPY at 112.660 remains highly bid, supported by carry and monetary divergence. Watch the next RBA policy meeting on 11 Aug to confirm the hawkish trajectory.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options Skew + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 162.379, holding well above the critical 160.00 level that previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings, elevating unilateral intervention risk. Speculative positioning is heavily short JPY with COT LF net short at -137,828 (25.3% long), while retail sentiment is also extremely short USD/JPY at 83% short. However, the options market is actively hedging JPY strength, with the 25d Risk Reversal showing a strong put skew of -1.52, signaling institutional demand for USD/JPY downside protection. Fresh US military strikes on Iran introduce geopolitical risk, but the +265bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.98%) keeps the pair bid. The options skew suggests smart money is positioning for a sharp reversal via intervention or safe-haven JPY repatriation if Middle East tensions escalate further. Watch for direct BoJ action or a break below 161.50 to confirm a trend reversal.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Volatility Premium
signals.text: USD/CAD exhibits a severe cross-asset correlation break against WTI Oil, with the rolling correlation collapsing to -0.865 vs a 252-day norm of +0.055 (a -2.08σ deviation). This decoupling occurs as WTI rises 2.21% to $72.0, which would typically support the CAD, but USD/CAD remains elevated at 1.4200 due to a +132bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs CORRA 2.31%) and a negative Canadian Economic Surprise Index (CESI net -7). Speculators are heavily short CAD with COT LF net short at -92,861 (20.6% long), while retail is fighting the trend with 84% short USD/CAD. The options market is pricing in significant uncertainty, with AUD/USD and USD/CHF showing extreme VRPs, while USD/CAD's structural divergence from oil suggests carry and data divergence dominate commodity flows. Watch the BoC policy meeting on 15 Jul to see if a dovish stance validates the CAD weakness.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Geopolitical Headwinds
signals.text: EUR/USD is holding at 1.14090, struggling to maintain ground above 1.1400 as fresh US strikes on Iran drive safe-haven USD demand ahead of the FOMC Minutes. The options market shows an elevated volatility risk premium, with EUR/USD 1M implied volatility at 7.1% vs a realized HV30 of 4.8% (+2.3% VRP), indicating strong hedging demand over realized price action. This defensive posture is supported by a persistent negative Eurozone Economic Surprise Index (CESI net -10) and a structural +145bp OIS carry deficit (SOFR 3.63% vs €STR 2.18%) favoring the USD. Speculators remain net short EUR (COT LF net -83,016), aligning with the options market's cautious stance. Watch the FOMC Minutes release later today as the primary catalyst for a break of the 1.1400 support level.
